session_summary: Session executed the IC calibration-ladder arc (preregister rungs 1-3, run the new ladder script, land @sec-ic) and then Marcus zoomed out to audit the paper's overall standing. His role was strategic: he demanded reproducibility and verification evidence, asked what was actually proven and why it matters, supplied a Schafer normalization identity that explained the residual O(1) term, scoped the next session to a non-nested CS-vs-AR(1) comparison, and asked for the kickoff prompt.

coded_turns.quote:
the calibration-ladder rungs 1–3 are now IN-PAPER — this session's deliverable is the IC application
  WITH the first/second/all-moment matching contrast, preregistered before any code.
coded_turns.note: Detailed resume brief: ladder in-paper, preregister-before-code discipline, locked results, guardrails, finish criteria.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: todo/027 ladder prereg; @sec-ic landing; D-06 scope split

coded_turns.note: Operational nudge to retry after an API socket error; no substantive signal.
Second retry nudge after another API socket error; routine.

coded_turns.quote: What evidence do we have that our santorra bentler or equiquantile is working?
coded_turns.note: Demands verification evidence that the SB and equipercentile ladder rungs actually calibrate.

coded_turns.quote: Is everything in a way that is reproducible?
coded_turns.note: Imposes reproducibility check on the ladder run; led to prereg-before-code rule + CLAUDE.md Commands entry.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: CLAUDE.md Commands ladder entry; todo/027 reproducibility note

coded_turns.quote: So beyond the bias term. What have we "proven" in the derivations that is improtant and whhy is it important?
coded_turns.note: Reframes to interrogate what is actually proven and its significance beyond the headline bias.

coded_turns.quote: What are the four key messages we are going to hand the reader at the end of the day, and where are we in terms of delivering multimodal, well-sourced evidence that we are correct.
coded_turns.note: Zooms out to demand the four reader takeaways and an evidence-completeness audit.

coded_turns.quote: On M2, is it possible that the O(1) is the integrated constant that noramlizes a posterior distribution? i.e., logP(Ym,Yo|\theta) = const. + logP(Yo|\theta) + logP(Ym|Yo,\theta)?
coded_turns.note: Proposes the residual O(1) is the posterior normalization constant via the factorization identity.

coded_turns.quote: By the way that equation si taken from Schafer
coded_turns.note: Attributes the factorization identity to Schafer, supplying the literature source for the M2 argument.

coded_turns.quote: So do we ahve all the results we need to write an effective paper in reproducible way? Or do we just need to do the full run?
coded_turns.note: Checks whether evidence base is complete and reproducible, isolating the remaining full-run dependency.

coded_turns.quote: I agree with the nonnested. I think that needs to be the focus of the next session. What nonnested can we do? compound symmetry vs. ar1 autocorrelation?
coded_turns.note: Ratifies non-nested as next focus and proposes the concrete CS-vs-AR(1) comparison pair.

coded_turns.quote: Give me the prompt that starts the next session.
coded_turns.note: Requests the handoff/kickoff prompt for the next session.

ai_side.evidence: a rerun clobbers the committed caches — it bit me during the determinism check just now; I `git restore`d them
ai_side.note: Assistant flagged a reproducibility hazard it had just hit (cache overwrite on rerun).

ai_side.evidence: L1–L4 are derived at physics-of-the-problem rigor ... not Annals-grade formal proofs with uniform-integrability bookkeeping
ai_side.note: Assistant volunteered an honesty caveat on the evidence grade of 'proven'.

ai_side.evidence: we won't let a noise floor masquerade as a null result
ai_side.note: Assistant flagged the (A)+(C) differential's unknown magnitude and a pilot-based power gate.

ai_side.evidence: if the differential's *sign* is cheaply derivable ... the prereg can state a signed prediction ... That's the first decision of the preregistration.
ai_side.note: Assistant surfaced the signed-vs-two-sided prediction choice as the first prereg decision.
